title: Secession of Ando
content:

{{Quote|It's clear where home is now|Aqualish [[Unga Torr]] and his family before their departure from Coruscant|<ref name="NEW45.2">{{Hnn|45|news/13228_2.html|Mass Aqualish Exodus Expected}}</ref>}}
The '''Secession of Ando''' was one of the defining events of the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]], occurring on the eve of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] in [[23 BBY/Legends|23 BBY]]. The [[Aqualish/Legends|Aqualish]] of the planet [[Ando/Legends|Ando]] unilaterally declared their withdrawal from the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], led by fervent nationalist leaders and rallying cries for independence. Ando became a reliable stronghold of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], supporting the fight against Republic forces in the region.

==History==
===Prelude===
[[Ando/Legends|Ando]], an oceanic planet in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]], was the homeworld of the warlike [[Aqualish/Legends|Aqualish]] species. Many millennia earlier, their expansion had been [[Aqualish–Republic War|crushed]] by the far more technologically advanced [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], which was then extending its borders. Ando was forced to abandon its conquests but was offered instead a constructive path of development, being guided in solving its internal problems, and soon the planet became part of the interstellar hegemony.<ref name="NG">''[[The New Essential Guide to Alien Species]]''</ref> However, some Aqualish refused to accept foreign interference.<ref name="CE">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> For a long time, Ando remained loyal to the Republic and, whatever [[Sith Empire/Legends|threats]] of the [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Dark Side]] loomed over the galaxy, it always returned to the Republic’s fold.<ref name="EA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> This could not last forever, and the point of fracture is usually traced to the period of the [[Andoan Wars/Legends|Andoan Wars]], which raged during the latter half of the Republic’s final century. This local war in the [[Lambda sector/Legends|Lambda sector]], essentially a colonial struggle of Aqualish against Aqualish, did not initially attract the close attention of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]’s authorities – at least not until the [[Battle of Raquish|disastrous battle]] of [[Raquish system/Legends|Raquish]]. The brutal tactics and terrifying firepower shocked the distant Republic, which immediately authorized the deployment of the [[Judicial Forces/Legends|Judicial Department’s fleet]] into the sector.<ref name="EW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref> The conflict was forcibly suppressed, compelling the signing of the [[Treaty of Raquish|treaty of enforced demilitarization]] of the region.<ref name="IC67">{{InsiderCite|67|CIS Shadowfeed Dispatch 14:4:12 Edition}}</ref> While the peace settlement favored the [[Andoan Free Colonies/Legends|planetary communities]] of Aqualish who wished to govern themselves independently, the Aqualish of the Andoan metropolis regarded it as a humiliating insult to their dignity. This, in turn, pushed the population and the authorities of the planet toward seeking a radical resolution to the perceived injustice.<ref name="EW" />

===Separatist Crisis===
{{Quote|Ando will now determine her own future. It is meant to be, given the rampant hypocrisy of Palpatine's Republic. The current dispute over the creation of a Republic army is just another in a long line of insults to the Aqualish people|[[First Minister (Ando)|First Minister]] [[Daragi Hoba]] is giving a speech to the crowd|<ref name="NEW45">{{Hnn|45|news/13228_1.html|Ando, Sy Myrth Secede}}</ref>}}
[[File:PoNudo-WOSW.png|thumb|left|200px|Po Nudo, who marked Ando's secession from the Galactic Republic]]
For more than fifteen years, the Aqualish had no choice but to unquestioningly obey the Republic’s restrictions, even though this often led to bloody incidents, such as the [[Horos Spine Incident|episode]] on the [[Horos Spine]]. [[Senator/Legends|Ando’s representative]] in the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]], the Aqualish [[Po Nudo/Legends|Po Nudo]], was not inspired by the ideals of a unified state and shared his people’s disdain for Coruscant’s dominance.<ref name="NEW45" /> War was looming in the galaxy, and the final months before the [[Clone Wars/Legends|collapse]] of the apparent peace were a chaotic tangle of intrigue, secret alliances, and betrayals. In [[23 BBY/Legends|23 BBY]], three months before the outbreak of large-scale conflict,<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> a turning point – or convenient pretext – emerged: the debate over the [[Military Creation Act/Legends|Military Creation Act]], a bill to reestablish the Republic’s standing army in response to the growing [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|separatist movement]] across many worlds. During a morning Senate session, representative Po Nudo, through a trusted [[Droid/Legends|droid]] proxy, formally declared the independence of his homeworld and tendered his resignation.<ref name="NEW45" />

Less than two standard [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]] after the landmark announcement, jubilant anti-Republic crowds flooded the busy [[Raquish Thoroughfare]] in Ando’s capital, [[Quantill City]]. The spontaneous celebrations drew in Aqualish of all kinds, regardless of ethnic origin or place of residence. Festive throngs gathered in the city square, holding placards condemning the Republic and praising their beloved senator, while several effigies of the Republic [[Senate Guard/Legends|Senate Guards]] were burned. Through a [[Hologram/Legends|holographic]] projection, proud [[First Minister (Ando)|First Minister]] [[Daragi Hoba]] addressed the people. The politician did nothing to temper the public fervor but instead further inflamed the initial passion with a nationalist statement. Po Nudo himself was not seen on Ando during the celebrations, and he was likely en route, returning from Coruscant.<ref name="NEW45" />

Although Ando’s secession surprised few, [[Journalist/Legends|correspondents]] turned to the [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Chancellor’s]] office for comment on the events, but none received a reply. Journalists merely speculated that the Aqualish defection would be addressed at the next day’s Senate session.<ref name="NEW45" /> The [[HoloNet News/Legends|HoloNet News]] agency conducted a poll asking, «Could the Ando separation have been prevented?», to which only 22 percent of respondents answered in the affirmative.<ref name="NEW45.3">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|45||Quick Poll}}</ref> Shortly afterward, star systems such as [[Umbara system/Legends|Umbara]], [[Thustra system/Legends|Thustra]], and [[Persavi system/Legends|Persavi]] followed the lead of [[Antar 4/Legends|Antar]], Ando, and Sy Myrth in breaking with the Republic.<ref name="EW" />

===Aftermath===
====Refugees crisis====
{{Quote|I can't tell you how appreciative our family is. But as beautiful as it is here, it's not home, and I hope that I can return to the seas of Ando someday and find the rest of my family|Aqualish Aqualish expatriate [[Orogor Binn]], on [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]]|<ref name="NEW49">{{Hnn|49|news/13328_2.html|Alderaan Opens to Refugee Relief Movement}}</ref>}}
[[File:Westport.jpg|thumb|right|200px|An Andoan family awaits departure from Westport]]
In the immediate aftermath of Ando’s declaration, «[[Freespan Starlanes Transport]]» announced plans to double the number of regular passenger routes to the planet, anticipating a surge in travel among Aqualish expatriates who were now likely to seek reunion with their distant home. Observers expected that interstellar carriers such as «[[Romodi Interstellar]]» and «[[Galaxy Tours/Legends|Galaxy Tours]]» would soon announce similar measures. Less than a day after Ando’s secession, bookings made by Aqualish passengers departing Coruscant had already surpassed five hundred. The emigration flow moved primarily through [[Westport]], and while traffic had previously spiked by six thousand percent since the start of the Separatist Crisis, it was now largely Aqualish families leaving the world together. With the growing number of one-way flights, economic analysts predicted a grim outlook for the [[Galactic City/Legends|Coruscanti ecumenopolis]] district of [[Taung Heights]], whose livelihood was tightly bound to the planet’s largest Aqualish diaspora. According to the official census, some 145 million Aqualish resided on Coruscant, with the greatest concentration in Taung Heights. While Westport struggled with the mass Aqualish exodus, security services at [[Eastport/Legends|Eastport]] – already strained by the nonstop influx of refugees from seceding systems – warned to prepare for incoming Aqualish [[Loyalist/Legends|loyal]] to the Republic.<ref name="NEW45.2" /> Sympathetic Mid Rim worlds eased immigration restrictions,<ref name="NEW52">{{Hnn|52|regional/13418_1.html|Mid Rim Worlds Open Ports to Refugees}}</ref> while the government of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]] allocated unused lands for settlement, drawing Aqualish from Ando either in search of a new home or simply a refuge from turbulent times.<ref name="NEW49" />

====Political impact====
{{Quote|Some of us still have a sense of loyalty to the Republic, and do not want to be branded as traitors. I will be there, on Coruscant, on the 16th, to make our voices heard. I believe in the Republic and in the Senate, and do not look to run away like the cowards in the Old World.|Senator [[Gorothin Vagger]]|<ref name="HNN49.2">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|49|regional/13328_1.html|Andoan Free Colonies Criticize Aqualish}}</ref>}}
Despite Ando’s withdrawal from the Republic, the [[Andoan Free Colonies/Legends|free-minded government]] of the former Aqualish colonies maintained its allegiance to the galactic power. Their leadership convened a demonstrative [[Andosha II loyalty summit|loyalty summit]] on [[Andosha II]], where they unequivocally denounced the arrogance of their homeworld. The colonies’ representative, [[Gorothin Vagger]], branded the Andoan Aqualish as cowards and pledged not to neglect Senate sessions.<ref name="HNN49.2" />

[[File:Farr.jpg|thumb|left|230px|Senator Onaconda Farr makes serious accusations regarding Republic security]]
Soon after, the senator from [[Rodia/Legends|Rodia]], [[Onaconda Farr/Legends|Onaconda Farr]], conducted a personal investigation into the activities of hundreds of [[Politics/Legends|politicians]] and [[Judge/Legends|judges]] who had shaped the Republic’s agenda and compiled a list of names exposed for their ties to Separatist worlds. In particular, senator [[Havriso Looruya]], a member of the Chancellor’s inner-circle [[Loyalist Committee/Legends|Loyalist Committee]], had been transferring large sums of [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] to Separatist accounts on Ando, [[Sy Myrth/Legends|Sy Myrth]], and [[Sluis Van/Legends|Sluis Van]]. The disgraced [[Senate Bureau of Intelligence/Legends|Senate Bureau of Intelligence]] used Farr’s list to continue its inquiry.<ref name="NEW54">{{Hnn|54|news/1352_2.html|Rodian Senator Uncovers Loyalist-Separatist Ties}}</ref> A [[Standard week/Legends|week]] after the scandal, senator Onaconda took Looruya’s place on the Committee, while the Bureau’s investigation resulted in a further 12 arrests.<ref name="NEW55">{{Hnn|55|news/1359_4.html|Senator Farr Named to Loyalist Committee}}</ref>

Po Nudo himself, who had proclaimed Ando’s independence, actively established ties with the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatists]]. In return for his cooperation and the planet’s rich resources, the [[InterGalactic Banking Clan/Legends|InterGalactic Banking Clan]] and the [[Techno Union/Legends|Techno Union]] appointed Nudo as head of the [[Hyper-Communications Cartel/Legends|Hyper-Communications Cartel]], which oversaw the [[Shadowfeed/Legends|Shadowfeed]], a counterpart to the HoloNet, was used to spread Separatist ideas throughout territories under Republic control.<ref name="FS">''[[Star Wars: The Complete Visual Dictionary]]''</ref>

Up to that point Ando had not been stripped of its planetary defense forces, and it embarked on a course of rapid rearmament. Within weeks of secession, the world had acquired from its new allies – among other things – [[Frigate/Legends|frigates]] and [[Droid starfighter/Legends|droid starfighters]] to secure command of the surrounding space. News of these developments [[Kendra Tissido|reached]] the Senate, alarming many, particularly senator [[Rasit Brun]], who represented the neighboring world of [[Rintonne/Legends|Rintonne]]. He interpreted the arming of the Aqualish as an act of war and accused the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] of sponsoring aggression.<ref name="NEW50">{{Hnn|50|regional/1344_2.html|Shots Fired in Andoan Space}}</ref>

With the advent of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the [[Commission for the Protection of the Republic/Legends|Commission for the Protection of the Republic]] was rapidly formed in order to secure the survival of the Republic in testing times. The committee, however, would bully government officials into leaning their way, and the [[First Minister (Coruscant Ministry of Ingress)/Legends|First Minister]] of the [[Coruscant Ministry of Ingress/Legends|Coruscant Ministry of Ingress]] [[Tannon Praji/Legends|Tannon Praji]] was no exception. COMPOR forced Praji to deport those whose [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworlds]] had sided with the Confederacy,<ref name="db">{{DB|character|tannonpraji|Praji, Tannon}}</ref> such as vast communities of [[Quarren/Legends|Quarren]], [[Aqualish/Legends|Aqualish]], [[Koorivar/Legends|Koorivar]], [[Neimoidian/Legends|Neimoidian]], [[Gossam/Legends|Gossam]] and more. The goal was never fully enforceable,<ref name="ttsp">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/source/praji/20090205.html|text=Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Praji|int=Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Praji}}</ref> but Praji's efforts were enough to make an impact, the results of which for the First Minister were twofold. Chancellor Palpatine praised Praji's efforts and movements, claiming that they were instrumental in securing peace and security on the capital. However, many of Praji's critics were pushed to extreme measures, and several [[Assassin/Legends|assassination]] and kidnapping attempts were made on the First Minister by both dissidents and agents of the Confederacy.<ref name="db" /><ref name="ttsp" /> Following one particular attempt by Aqualish Separatists, Praji was offered the protection services of [[Ottegru Grey]] by the Bank of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core]], at a nominal fee.<ref name="ttsp" />

At the same time, tensions among the Aqualish factions in the Lambda sector reached their peak, erupting into the [[Battle of Ando|open conflict]] during the Clone Wars.<ref name="NEC" /> However, aggressive and violent, the Aqualish enjoyed the onset of the conflict.<ref name="MCW">{{SWMiniCite|set=Star Wars: The Clone Wars|pack=Aqualish Warrior}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The secession of Ando in the pre-war years of the Republic was first covered in a [[HoloNet News (original website)|HoloNet news]] report, ''[[HoloNet News Vol. 531 45]]'', where it was the central event. The declaration of the planet’s independence was carried out through legal channels and political mechanisms, and the Republic in no way interfered in the process, merely resigning itself to the final decision of Ando’s authorities. Nevertheless, the ''[[Essential Atlas]]'' falsely claims that the secession did not occur without violence and bloodshed, similar to the [[Battle of Antar 4 (Separatist Crisis)|coup]] on [[Antar 4/Legends|Antar 4]] during the same period.
